description: Authoritative LazySitter roster. Single source of truth for both the Claude Code and Codex adapters. The installer derives per-tool output from this file plus core/agents/*.md. Do not hand-edit installed copies; edit here and re-run `lazysitter update`.

tiers.high: Most capable model. Deep design, adversarial reasoning, intent judgement.
tiers.high_alt: A DISTINCT high-capability model from `high`, used for red-team so it does not share the implementers' / architect's blind spots. Falls back to `high` if unset.
tiers.mid: Balanced model. Most build and verification work.
tiers.low: Fast, cheap model. Mechanical scans and narrow classification.

lazysitter-business-analyst.note: Writes ONLY its own REQUIREMENT.md to the run dir (self-persist); prompt-scoped, never edits source.

lazysitter-explorer.note: Writes ONLY its own CONTEXT-PACK.md to the run dir (self-persist); prompt-scoped, never edits source.

lazysitter-spec-writer.note: Writes ONLY its own ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.md to the run dir (self-persist); prompt-scoped, never edits source.

lazysitter-architect.note: Writes ONLY its own PLAN.md/DECISIONS.md/LIMITATIONS.md to the run dir (self-persist); prompt-scoped, never edits source.

lazysitter-dependency-auditor.note: May need network for CVE/registry lookups; enable network in the codex sandbox config if required.

lazysitter-test-author.note: Writes tests only. Spawned in parallel with implementers so it is structurally blind to implementation.

lazysitter-red-team.note: Uses the `high_alt` model slot to avoid shared blind spots with the build lineage.

lazysitter-release-agent.note: Mutates git at the merge gate. Requires approval unless autoApproveGit is set.

lazysitter-rollback-agent.note: Standing revert authority in the monitoring window. Mutates git.
